---
title: "Society & Well-being"
description: "Life satisfaction and what underlies it — Canada and OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import os
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar, single_line
```

Data from the [World Happiness Report](https://worldhappiness.report/), based on
the Gallup World Poll. The happiness score (Life Evaluation) is a national
average of responses to the Cantril ladder question, where people rate their
current life from 0 (worst possible) to 10 (best possible). Scores shown are
three-year averages.

## Happiness Score (Life Evaluation)

```{python}
#| echo: false
whr_path = "data/wellbeing/whr_happiness.csv"
if os.path.exists(whr_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(whr_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "happiness_score",
        title="", yaxis_title="Life Evaluation (0–10)", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: World Happiness Report (Gallup World Poll) | Data as of: {get_data_date(whr_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Happiness data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest Happiness Score: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(whr_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(whr_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "happiness_score", "Life Evaluation (0–10)",
        f"Source: World Happiness Report | Data as of: {get_data_date(whr_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.2f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## What Underlies the Score

The World Happiness Report uses statistical analysis to estimate how much of each
country's score is associated with six factors plus a baseline ("Dystopia +
residual"). The bars below decompose the latest score for each country — a way to
see *which* factors are doing the work, not just the total.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(whr_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(whr_path)
    latest_year = df["year"].max()
    latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].sort_values("happiness_score").copy()

    components = [
        ("explained_gdp", "GDP per capita", "#1f77b4"),
        ("explained_social_support", "Social support", "#2ca02c"),
        ("explained_life_expectancy", "Healthy life expectancy", "#ff7f0e"),
        ("explained_freedom", "Freedom", "#9467bd"),
        ("explained_generosity", "Generosity", "#8c564b"),
        ("explained_corruption", "Low corruption", "#17becf"),
        ("dystopia_residual", "Dystopia + residual", "#cccccc"),
    ]

    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in components:
        if col in latest.columns:
            fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
                y=latest["country_name"], x=latest[col].fillna(0),
                name=name, orientation="h", marker_color=color,
                hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{x:.2f}}<extra>%{{y}}</extra>",
            ))

    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack",
        xaxis_title="Contribution to Life Evaluation score",
        yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.08, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
        height=650, margin=dict(b=120, t=20),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: World Happiness Report ({latest_year}) | Data as of: {get_data_date(whr_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.22,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Happiness data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Safety

Safety is a basic component of well-being, and crime is often discussed using individual incidents rather than long-run trends. The two measures below give the trend instead. The **Crime Severity Index** weights each crime by its seriousness (so a homicide counts far more than a minor theft) and is indexed to 2006 = 100. The **homicide rate** is the most consistently measured serious crime over the long run.

```{python}
#| echo: false
csi_path = "data/wellbeing/statcan_crime_severity.csv"
if os.path.exists(csi_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(csi_path, parse_dates=["date"])
    fig = single_line(df, "date", "crime_severity_index", title="",
                      yaxis_title="Crime Severity Index (2006 = 100)", rangeslider=True,
                      source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 35-10-0026-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(csi_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Crime severity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
hom_path = "data/wellbeing/statcan_homicide_rate.csv"
if os.path.exists(hom_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(hom_path, parse_dates=["date"])
    fig = single_line(df, "date", "homicide_rate", title="",
                      yaxis_title="Homicides per 100,000 population", rangeslider=True,
                      source_note=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 35-10-0068-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(hom_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Homicide data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Crime Severity by City

The same Crime Severity Index, mapped across metropolitan areas (latest year) — it weights offences by seriousness, so a city with more serious crime scores higher. **Zoom and pan to your area.** Reported for larger metropolitan areas only (smaller places aren't shown).

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map
cma_geo_path = "data/geo/cma_2021.geojson"
cma_path = "data/geo/statcan_cma_indicators.csv"
if os.path.exists(cma_geo_path) and os.path.exists(cma_path):
    cma_geo = json.load(open(cma_geo_path))
    cma = pd.read_csv(cma_path, dtype={"cmauid": str})
    sub = cma.dropna(subset=["crime_severity"])
    fig = choropleth_map(cma_geo, sub, "cmauid", "crime_severity", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="Crime Severity Index", value_fmt=".1f", colorscale="YlOrRd",
        zmin=float(sub["crime_severity"].quantile(0.05)), zmax=float(sub["crime_severity"].quantile(0.95)),
        source_note="Source: Statistics Canada, Table 35-10-0026-01 (latest year). Larger metro areas only.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("City crime map not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_census_geo`.")
```

## Download Data

- [Happiness Scores & Factor Decomposition (CSV)](../data/wellbeing/whr_happiness.csv) — World Happiness Report (Gallup World Poll)
- [City indicators incl. Crime Severity (CSV)](../data/geo/statcan_cma_indicators.csv) — Statistics Canada (2021 Census + Table 35-10-0026-01)
- [Crime Severity Index (CSV)](../data/wellbeing/statcan_crime_severity.csv) — Statistics Canada Table 35-10-0026-01
- [Homicide Rate (CSV)](../data/wellbeing/statcan_homicide_rate.csv) — Statistics Canada Table 35-10-0068-01
